summary:
The patient should undergo an urgent high inguinal orchidectomy since he has a palpable testicle mass associated with significantly raised alpha-fetoprotein levels suggestive of germinoma; this approach provides accurate diagnosis while allowing prompt initiation of appropriate therapy without delay compared to alternative procedures like needle aspiration which carry higher risks such as seeding metastases through punctures. Given his condition matches indications supporting radical surgery promptly versus watchful waiting especially when there isn't strong evidence against malignant potential warranting more cautious management options first. To ensure proper surgical planning and execution involving experienced urologists familiarized specifically within these complex cases would be ideal but not necessarily required if local expertise exists otherwise prioritizing timely care outweighs perfect conditions being met exactly before acting decisively based upon available information at hand today regarding optimal therapeutic strategies moving ahead immediately post-consultation discussion amongst multidisciplinary team members involved directly caring about said individual case presented herein now.

Alpha-Fetoprotein indicating probable germ-cell cancer; opting instead for milder interventions could pose greater risk owing primarily from possible dissemination via fine needles used elsewhere thus making thorough excision preferable even though specialized skills may occasionally prove lacking locally yet still essential overall benefit justifies expeditious implementation regardless minor Given his condition matches indications supporting radical surgery promptly versus watchful waiting especially when there isn't strong evidence against malignant potential warranting more cautious management options first.
